title: tylophora macrantha
intro: Tylophora macrantha, also known as the "Tylophora vine" or "Indian ipecac," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and India, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as tylophorine and tylophorinine. The health benefits of Tylophora macrantha include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Tylophora macrantha include teas, decoctions, and extracts, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
